static_proof.notes: No source files changed. Constitutional evidence assembly only.

runtime_proof.note: T2 verification — no additional DB queries required; input evidence bundles from UTV2-1217/1218/1219 are SHA-bound and already verified against live Supabase.

d_const_5_resolution.resolution_statement: Scoring is no longer a pure market-consensus echo. Wave 5 (UTV2-1211–1215) wired five independent stat-based feature modules (matchup-context, player-form, opportunity, efficiency, game-context) into computeStatProjection alongside devig. The scoring path now incorporates stat-model projections as a distinct signal from market consensus.
d_const_5_resolution.empirical_evidence_note: UTV2-1217 found insufficient volume for statistical CLV/edge claim — all 503 graded post-Wave-5 picks with devig data have sport_id=NULL (synthetic smoke-test corpus, not live production picks). Empirical CLV/edge evidence deferred to UTV2-1042 (state:data-gated) when real production picks accumulate under the Wave-5 path.

UTV2-1217.key_finding: No CLV/edge claim supportable — corpus is synthetic/smoke-test (sport_id=NULL). Wave-5 code merged but champion models remain v0.1-baseline-2026-04-22 with artifact_sha=NULL. Follow-up evidence collection required once real production picks accumulate.

UTV2-1218.key_finding: R2 determinism confirmed: computeStatProjection is deterministic across NBA/NFL/MLB/NHL. output_hash_run_1 === output_hash_run_2. Structural property verified by SHA-256 comparison.

UTV2-1219.key_finding: V-R4 fault injection complete. 74 tests pass. Fail-closed on opportunity/efficiency/snap_share/line guards. Five validation gaps documented (NaN propagation, zero home_away_factor, negative variance clamp).

nan_efficiency_projection.finding: NaN is not caught by the <= 0 guard (NaN <= 0 is false in JS); propagates to expected_value
nan_efficiency_projection.rationale: Input validation hardening required at the computeStatProjection boundary. Tracked as follow-up hardening issue. Not a data corruption risk in current pipeline (inputs are constructed from typed domain objects), but is a latent correctness gap.
nan_efficiency_projection.follow_up: Open hardening issue: add NaN guard at efficiency_projection and stat_per_minute inputs

nan_home_away_factor.finding: NaN home_away_factor is not validated; propagates to expected_value
nan_home_away_factor.rationale: Same pattern as NaN efficiency_projection. home_away_factor is currently derived from schedule data and is structurally non-null in the live pipeline, but NaN is not explicitly rejected. Hardening issue required.
nan_home_away_factor.follow_up: Open hardening issue: add NaN/zero guard at home_away_factor input

zero_home_away_factor.finding: Zero home_away_factor is not validated; collapses expected_value to 0
zero_home_away_factor.rationale: Zero is a degenerate value (neutral home/away effect) but silently collapses output. Should be treated as an invalid sentinel and rejected. Same hardening issue as NaN.

negative_variance_components.finding: Negative volatility components are not rejected; Math.max(variance, 0.0001) clamps them
negative_variance_components.rationale: Math.max clamp is an intentional defensive floor — negative variance is physically impossible but can arise from floating-point subtraction. The clamp to 0.0001 produces a near-zero (not zero) variance, which is well-defined for the distribution functions downstream. This is correct defensive behavior. No hardening issue required.

nan_stat_per_minute.finding: NaN stat_per_minute is not validated; propagates to feature vector hash
nan_stat_per_minute.rationale: stat_per_minute is a derived field; NaN propagation would corrupt the feature hash silently. Input validation required.
nan_stat_per_minute.follow_up: Included in NaN input validation hardening issue
